questions.q: In the Puranas, the sage Markandeya is celebrated as a devotee of which deity?
questions.explanation: Markandeya was a devoted worshipper of Shiva, whose grace saved him from death.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, what fate was Markandeya destined for at the age of sixteen?
questions.explanation: Markandeya was destined to die when he reached sixteen years of age.

questions.q: The sage Markandeya is famed for being granted what blessing by Shiva?
questions.explanation: Shiva conquered Death on his behalf and granted Markandeya eternal youth as a chiranjivi (immortal).

questions.q: In the Markandeya story, which deity is the lord of death who came to claim the boy's life?
questions.explanation: Yama, the god of death, came to take Markandeya's life with his noose.

questions.q: As Yama approached, what sacred object did Markandeya cling to for protection?
questions.explanation: Markandeya embraced the Shiva linga in devotion as Yama cast his noose.

questions.q: Which Purana is traditionally framed as the discourse of the sage Markandeya?
questions.explanation: The Markandeya Purana is named for and framed as the teachings attributed to the sage Markandeya.

questions.q: The celebrated Devi Mahatmya, glorifying the Goddess, is contained within which text?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya forms a section of the Markandeya Purana.

questions.q: The term used for an immortal being like Markandeya in Hindu tradition is which of the following?
questions.explanation: A chiranjivi is a being blessed with extraordinarily long life or immortality, a status Markandeya holds.

questions.q: During the cosmic dissolution, the sage Markandeya is famed for beholding which divine child?
questions.explanation: Markandeya is renowned for seeing the infant Krishna floating upon the waters during the pralaya.

questions.q: In the famous vision, upon what was the infant Krishna seen resting amid the cosmic ocean witnessed by Markandeya?
questions.explanation: Markandeya beheld the infant Krishna lying upon a banyan (vata) leaf during the cosmic dissolution.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, who was the father of the sage Markandeya?
questions.explanation: Markandeya was born to the sage Mrikandu, from whom his name is derived.

questions.q: In the Puranas, what choice were Markandeya's parents offered concerning their future son?
options: A son who would be a king, or a sage
A son blessed by Vishnu, or one blessed by Shiva
A short-lived but virtuous son, or a long-lived but foolish son
A single son, or a hundred ordinary sons
questions.explanation: Mrikandu was offered either a virtuous, gifted son who would live only sixteen years, or a long-lived but dull son, and chose the former.

questions.q: When Yama cast his noose to take Markandeya, the Puranas relate that the loop fell partly upon what?
questions.explanation: As Markandeya clung to it, Yama's noose fell around both the sage and the Shiva linga, provoking Shiva's wrath.

questions.q: In the iconography associated with the rescue of Markandeya, Shiva is depicted in which fierce form that conquers death?
questions.explanation: Shiva as Kalantaka or Kalari, 'the ender of Death,' is the form shown subduing Yama to save Markandeya.

questions.q: Which weapon or implement is Yama traditionally said to wield against his victims, including in his approach to Markandeya?
questions.explanation: Yama is depicted wielding the pasha, the noose with which he binds and draws out life.

questions.q: The Markandeya Purana is counted among which traditional category of eighteen major Hindu texts?
questions.explanation: The Markandeya Purana is reckoned as one of the eighteen Mahapuranas.

questions.q: In the Devi Mahatmya within the Markandeya Purana, the Goddess is celebrated chiefly for slaying which buffalo demon?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya glorifies the Goddess Durga's victory over the buffalo demon Mahishasura.

questions.q: The story of Markandeya's deliverance from death is most central to the worship of which deity?
questions.explanation: Markandeya's rescue, achieved by Shiva subduing Yama, is a celebrated testament to Shiva's grace.

questions.q: When Markandeya beheld the cosmic ocean during the pralaya, what surrounded him on all sides?
questions.explanation: During the cosmic dissolution Markandeya wandered amid a single boundless ocean of waters.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the great length of Markandeya's life is often expressed by saying he lived through many of which vast cosmic cycles?
questions.explanation: As a chiranjivi, Markandeya is said to witness the turning of yugas and kalpas, the great cosmic ages.

questions.q: The hymn called the Mrityunjaya or Mahamrityunjaya, invoked for protection from untimely death, is addressed to which deity revered in the Markandeya tradition?
questions.explanation: The Mahamrityunjaya mantra invokes Shiva, the conqueror of death whose grace saved Markandeya.

questions.q: Within the Mahabharata, the sage Markandeya appears chiefly in what role to the Pandavas?
options: A narrator of ancient tales and discourses
questions.explanation: In the Mahabharata, Markandeya visits the exiled Pandavas and recounts to them many sacred stories and teachings.

questions.q: In the Puranic account, at what age was Markandeya destined to die before Shiva intervened, a number echoed in his depiction as ever-youthful?
questions.explanation: Markandeya was fated to die at sixteen, and Shiva's boon fixed him at that youthful age forever.

questions.q: According to tradition, which boon-granting deity originally offered Mrikandu the choice that led to Markandeya's short-but-brilliant life?
questions.explanation: In the Puranic narrative it was Shiva who, pleased with Mrikandu's penance, offered the fateful choice of son.

questions.q: The vision of the infant Krishna on a banyan leaf granted to Markandeya is most elaborately recounted in which text?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates Markandeya's vision of the divine child upon the leaf amid the cosmic waters.

questions.q: In Hindu cosmology, the great flood through which Markandeya wandered and beheld Krishna marks which event?
options: The burning of the Khandava forest
questions.explanation: Markandeya's wandering upon the waters takes place during the pralaya, the dissolution at the close of a cosmic age.

questions.q: Markandeya is most often grouped together with which class of long-lived beings in Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Markandeya is traditionally counted among the chiranjivis, the immortals such as Hanuman, Parashurama, and Ashwatthama.

questions.q: The opening verses of the Devi Mahatmya, framed within the Markandeya Purana, recount the tale of which king who sought refuge with a sage?
questions.explanation: The Devi Mahatmya begins with King Suratha and the merchant Samadhi seeking the Goddess's grace through a sage's counsel.

questions.q: In Shaiva iconography of the Kalantaka episode, Shiva is shown emerging from the linga to strike down whom in defense of Markandeya?
questions.explanation: In the Kalantaka form, Shiva bursts from the linga to subdue Yama and rescue his devotee Markandeya.

questions.q: The name 'Markandeya' is patronymically derived from the name of his father, the sage Mrikandu, who himself was a descendant of which lineage?
options: The line of the sage Bhrigu
The line of the sage Vishwamitra
The line of the sage Vasishtha
The line of the sage Gautama
questions.explanation: Mrikandu, and thus Markandeya, is traditionally counted within the Bhrigu lineage of sages.
